channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Salesforce chief says he no longer backs Trump sending National Guard to San Francisco
item.description: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff apologized Friday for comments suggesting that President Trump should deploy the National Guard to San Francisco, saying he no longer believes it is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: AI-powered Bay Area home insurance startup expands in risky markets
item.description: San Francisco-based Stand uses remote sensing data and collects details from homeowners. The startup analyzes this information using AI, which it uses to simulate how wind, heat, embers and other factors stand to contribute to possible damage.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: A mini-CrowdStrike moment? Windows 11 update cripples dev environments
item.description: Just as Windows 10 has reached its end of life (EOL), issues with Windows 11 abound, the most recent an update that broke localhost for some developers, preventing them from accessing web apps running locally on their machines.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to make workers happier with less pay
item.description: Workers want remote work so badly they’re willing to take a 25% pay cut to get it, according to a new study from economists at Harvard University, Brown University, and UCLA. The study published this week was conducted with Levels.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta adds parental controls for AI-teen interactions
item.description: Meta is adding parental controls to kids’ interactions with artificial intelligence chatbots — including the ability to turn off one-on-one chats with AI characters altogether.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AI pushes CIOs to redefine IT roles for the future
item.description: AI is already reshaping the IT job landscape, with layoffs and demand for AI skills creating a mismatched market for IT skills. But the technology is also contributing to possible contraction for IT workforces as enterprises IT leaders find themselves redefining IT roles in the wake of AI’s rise.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 2 weird but wonderful Android launchers
item.description: I think at this point, it’s safe to say I’m an Android launcher aficionado. (I think it’s also safe to both pat myself on the back and bop myself uponst the nose for coming up with such a delightfully dorky description. But I’ll save that for later.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: Intel news and insights | AMD news and insights With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: SpaceX launches the 11th test flight of its mega Starship rocket with another win
item.description: SpaceX launched another of its mammoth Starship rockets on a test flight Monday, successfully making it halfway around the world while releasing mock satellites like last time. Starship — the biggest and most powerful rocket ever built — thundered into the evening sky from the southern tip of Texas.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI partners with Broadcom to design its own AI chips
item.description: OpenAI said Monday it is working with chipmaker Broadcom to design its own artificial intelligence computer chips. The two California companies didn't disclose the financial terms of the deal but said they will start deploying the new racks of customized “AI accelerators” late next year.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inside Nvidia’s ‘grid-to-chip’ vision: How Vera Rubin and Spectrum-XGS push toward AI giga-factories
item.description: Nvidia will be front-and-center at this week’s Global Summit for members of the Open Compute Project (OCP), emphasizing its “grid-to-chip” philosophy.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ethernet vs Wi-Fi security comparison reveals surprising results for home users seeking protection
item.description: Expert analysis reveals whether wired Ethernet or wireless Wi-Fi connections are safer for home internet use, plus practical steps to secure your network from attackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Crypto spent millions to defeat Sherrod Brown and elect allies. It's ready for a repeat in 2026
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — At a five-star resort tucked in the mountains of Jackson Hole, Wyoming, the cryptocurrency industry was celebrating a historic start to the year on Capitol Hill. Its priorities were sailing through Congress with unusual speed and one senator did not hesitate to say why. Sen.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How malicious party invites target your inbox
item.description: Cybercriminals are using fake invitation emails to trick recipients into downloading malware and stealing personal information and data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
